title: The Scarlet Letter
author: Hawthorne, Nathaniel
summary: The Scarlet Letter opens with a Custom-House preface in which the narrator recounts employment at Salem’s Custom House, framing his discovery of papers that introduce the main tale. In seventeenth-century Puritan Boston, Hester Prynne is publicly punished for adultery, forced to wear the scarlet letter A. Her child Pearl, the minister, and a mysterious physician—posed as a leech—become entwined in moral, psychological, and social conflicts. Themes of sin, punishment, hypocrisy, and redemption unfold through interviews, public spectacles, and intimate vigils, as private guilt and revenge shape the characters’ destinies amid strict colonial society, and enduring consequences for conscience and community.
